evidence.reason: Link text is generic and does not explain the destination or action.

issues.suggested_fix: Use descriptive link text like "Download scholarship guidelines" instead of "click here."

rule.title: Link text is too generic
rule.why_it_matters: Students who navigate by a list of links hear 'click here' with no context and cannot tell links apart.
rule.manual_review_notes: Surrounding text may add context visually, but the link should still make sense on its own.
rule.static_check_limitations: The check only matches a small list of common generic phrases.

evidence.reason: Heading level jumps from h1 to h3.

issues.suggested_fix: Do not skip heading levels; move from h1 to h2 before h3.

rule.title: Heading level is skipped
rule.why_it_matters: Jumping from h1 to h3 breaks the page outline that screen reader users rely on to understand and navigate content.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the heading order still makes sense when read as an outline.
rule.static_check_limitations: The check follows document order only; visual layout may differ.

issues.message: Rendered text may have low contrast

evidence.reason: Computed foreground/background contrast is below the conservative 4.5:1 review threshold.

issues.suggested_fix: Increase foreground/background contrast and confirm the final design with manual review.

rule.title: Rendered text may have low contrast
rule.why_it_matters: Low-vision readers may not be able to read text when the computed foreground and background colors are too similar.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm the sampled colors match what users actually see, especially over gradients, images, and layered components.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check samples browser-computed colors and does not prove full WCAG color contrast compliance.

issues.message: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow

evidence.reason: A rendered element is wider than the narrow viewport or uses a large fixed pixel width.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace fixed-width layout with responsive sizing such as max-width: 100%.

rule.title: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow
rule.why_it_matters: Large fixed-width elements can force horizontal scrolling and hide content when users zoom or use narrow windows.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the wide element is essential and whether all content remains reachable without two-dimensional scrolling.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

issues.message: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator

evidence.name: query
evidence.reason: The focused element did not expose an obvious outline, box-shadow, or border focus style in computed CSS.

issues.suggested_fix: Provide a clear :focus or :focus-visible style with visible outline, border, or shadow.

rule.title: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator
rule.why_it_matters: Keyboard users with low vision need a visible indicator to know which control currently has focus.
rule.manual_review_notes: Manually tab through the page; some custom focus styles may be visible but not detected by this heuristic.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Focus indicator detection checks computed outline, border, and shadow heuristically and can miss custom visual treatments.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.
Low-vision browser checks are conservative heuristics and require manual review.

limitations: Browser mode approximates keyboard interaction but does not simulate a full screen reader.
Accessible names are estimated and require manual review.
Focus overlay shows observed Tab stops in this browser run.
It does not represent every assistive technology experience.

limitations: Low-vision checks use browser-computed styles and conservative heuristics.
Rendered color contrast checks do not prove full WCAG conformance.
Zoom/reflow uses a narrow viewport approximation rather than a full zoom compliance test.
Focus indicator detection may miss custom focus styles.

ai_scout.summary: Possible accessibility barriers found on the Coursera contact page

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some links may have generic text that does not explain their destination or action
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users who rely on screen readers or have cognitive disabilities may have difficulty understanding the purpose of these links
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of generic link text found, such as 'here' and 'Learn more'

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some headings may have skipped levels, which could disrupt the page's structure
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users who rely on screen readers or have visual impairments may have difficulty navigating the page
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: One instance of a skipped heading level found, from h1 to h3

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some text may have low contrast with its background, which could make it difficult to read
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users with visual impairments may have difficulty reading text with low contrast
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of low contrast text found, with a contrast ratio below the conservative 4.5:1 review threshold

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some content may not reflow properly when the page is zoomed in, which could make it difficult to read
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users with visual impairments may need to zoom in on the page to read the content
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of fixed or wide content found, which may prevent reflow

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some focused elements may not have a visible focus indicator, which could make it difficult for users to navigate the page
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users who rely on keyboard navigation may have difficulty understanding which element has focus
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of missing focus indicators found, including on input fields and links

outreach_notes: We suggest reviewing the Coursera contact page for potential accessibility barriers and making necessary improvements to ensure equal access for all users.
limitations: AI Scout findings are suggestions and need human review.
AI Scout output is not a confirmed accessibility finding.
AI Scout does not determine legal compliance, WCAG compliance, or screen-reader behavior.
AI Scout output is not a needs human review accessibility finding.
